purpose: Pure presentational debug control bar — receives debugStatus + 8 callback props (onContinue, onPause, onStepOver, onStepInto, onStepOut, onRestart, onStop, onLaunch) from IDEView and renders standard debugger buttons with state-driven enable/disable. Includes a script-path + args input row that gates the Launch button (Run). Per-spec button availability matrix: idle→only Launch enabled; running→only Pause + Stop; paused→Continue, StepOver, StepInto, StepOut, Restart, Stop; stopped→only Launch. Status dot color comes from CSS vars (var(--text-tertiary), var(--color-in-progress), var(--color-warning, #f59e0b)). Enter in either input field triggers handleLaunch.

exports.name: DebugToolbar
exports.signature: { debugStatus, onContinue, onPause, onStepOver, onStepInto, onStepOut, onRestart, onStop, onLaunch }

gotchas: All callbacks are optional — Run/Continue/etc. silently no-op if not provided. Caller MUST wire them or buttons appear active but do nothing.

scriptPath state is LOCAL to this component — it is NOT lifted to AppContext. After a Stop, the field retains its value but if the component unmounts (debug session ends, IDEView hides toolbar via debugSession.status !== 'idle' check), the value is lost. IDEView keeps a separate debugLaunchConfigRef for restart purposes.
Restart in IDEView calls debugStop().then(setTimeout(debugLaunch, 300)) — the 300ms delay is empirical; without it, V8 inspector may not have torn down before relaunch attempts to attach.
canLaunch requires (idle || stopped) AND non-empty trimmed scriptPath — running/paused never expose Launch.
patterns: Pure-prop component — no AppContext, no IPC. Reusable in isolation/storybook.
Status-driven boolean flags (isIdle/isRunning/isPaused/isStopped) computed at top of render, then per-button can* booleans derived.
Inline SVG icons for every action (Play, Pause, StepOver, StepInto, StepOut, Restart, Stop, Launch) — no external icon library.
conventions: Button CSS classes 'debug-toolbar-btn' base + '--restart' / '--stop' modifiers for color theming.
Input fields share 'debug-toolbar-input' class with --script / --args modifiers.
